title: San Francisco sues 10 major food manufacturers over ultra-processe
raw_response: {"rating": -0.6, "lean": "Center", "explanation": "This is a news report. Overall framing and attribution are neutral, with balanced inclusion of the lawsuit’s claims and industry pushback; however, embedded link headlines emphasizing the harms of ultra-processed foods and an equity-impact note create a slight left-leaning tilt. The net effect is near-center with minimal lean left, resulting in a Center rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) Editorial link headlines within the piece repeatedly highlight alleged harms of ultra-processed and convenience foods (addictiveness, links to chronic disease and cancer), which bolsters the lawsuit’s pro-regulatory narrative; (2) The author includes an equity frame (higher impact on minority and low-income communities) via selected attribution, aligning with left-leaning public health and social-justice emphasis; (3) Countervailing balance: the piece features responses from two industry trade groups and notes outreach to all defendant companies, providing structural balance; (4) The report cites CDC data and references a federal report under a Republican administration figure, adding cross-partisan context that tempers ideological tilt; (5) Throughout, the author uses neutral verbs and clear attribution (“alleges,” “accuses,” “claims”), avoiding endorsement of either side’s assertions in their own voice."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is a news report. Overall framing and attribution are neutral, with balanced inclusion of the lawsuit’s claims and industry pushback; however, embedded link headlines emphasizing the harms of ultra-processed foods and an equity-impact note create a slight left-leaning tilt. The net effect is near-center with minimal lean left, resulting in a Center rating. Detailed Analysis: (1) Editorial link headlines within the piece repeatedly highlight alleged harms of ultra-processed and convenience foods (addictiveness, links to chronic disease and cancer), which bolsters the lawsuit’s pro-regulatory narrative; (2) The author includes an equity frame (higher impact on minority and low-income communities) via selected attribution, aligning with left-leaning public health and social-justice emphasis; (3) Countervailing balance: the piece features responses from two industry trade groups and notes outreach to all defendant companies, providing structural balance; (4) The report cites CDC data and references a federal report under a Republican administration figure, adding cross-partisan context that tempers ideological tilt; (5) Throughout, the author uses neutral verbs and clear attribution (“alleges,” “accuses,” “claims”), avoiding endorsement of either side’s assertions in their own voice.
